interface: GET /api/v1/logs (Okta management REST API, official OpenAPI spec 2026.07.1)

auth.oauth_alternative: Bearer token with scope okta.logs.read (not exercised by these tests)

query_parameters.since: ISO 8601 lower bound (published time for bounded queries, persistence time for polling); default 7 days before until
query_parameters.until: ISO 8601 upper bound; a polling sweep MUST leave it unset
query_parameters.filter: SCIM-style expression, e.g. eventType eq "user.session.start" — URL-encoded on the wire
query_parameters.sortOrder: ASCENDING (default) or DESCENDING; polling requires ASCENDING
query_parameters.after: OPAQUE, system-generated cursor. Okta hands it back inside the Link rel="next" URL; clients must never craft or derive one — only echo a cursor previously received

pagination.link_header: every response carries Link rel="self"; polling queries (ASCENDING, no until) ALWAYS carry Link rel="next", even for an empty page
pagination.next_semantics: the next URL must be requested verbatim — the server may reorder params or add its own; rebuilding the URL loses them
pagination.sweep_termination: a page with zero events means the stream is drained for now
pagination.checkpoint: persist the after cursor extracted from a page's next link ONLY once every event of that page has been delivered downstream; a failed delivery must leave the previous checkpoint in place so the next sweep resumes with after={stored cursor} plus the original since/filter/limit/sortOrder

deduplication: event uuid is the unique event identifier; polling can repeat events at page/cursor boundaries and a resumed sweep refetches the failed page, so exporters must skip uuids already delivered

headers_on_every_response.X-Rate-Limit-Reset: UTC epoch seconds when the window resets

body.errorSummary: API call exceeded rate limit due to too many requests.

429.handling: wait until the reset time (computed against the response Date header, not the local clock), retry the SAME URL; after 3 total attempts surface a typed rate-limit error carrying the reset epoch
429.proactive: when a response reports Remaining=0, gate the NEXT request until the reset time

mock_fixture.org_base_url: loopback http://127.0.0.1:{port} standing in for https://{yourOktaDomain}
mock_fixture.api_token: 00dummySSWSlogt0ken-fixture-4Qz1 (dummy; must never appear in error messages)
mock_fixture.boundary_duplicate: evt-b2 appears as the last event of one page and the first of the next
mock_fixture.srvMarker: next links carry a srvMarker query param that only survives verbatim link-following
